steps.title: Find the slope of line s
steps.content: Rewrite $8x+2y=0$ in slope-intercept form: $$2y=-8x$$ $$y=-4x.$$ So line $s$ has slope $-4$.

steps.title: Use the perpendicular slope
steps.content: A line perpendicular to slope $-4$ has slope equal to the negative reciprocal, which is $\frac{1}{4}$. Line $t$ passes through $(6,0)$, so its equation is $$y=\frac{1}{4}(x-6).$$

steps.title: Use the given y-value
steps.content: The other point on line $t$ is $(k,\frac{5}{4})$, so plug in $y=\frac{5}{4}$: $$\frac{5}{4}=\frac{1}{4}(k-6).$$ Multiply by $4$ to get $5=k-6$, so $k=11$.

steps.title: Check the point
steps.content: At $x=11$, the line $y=\frac{1}{4}(x-6)$ gives $y=\frac{1}{4}(5)=\frac{5}{4}$. Therefore the point is $(11,\frac{5}{4})$, and the value of $k$ is 11.
